name: architecture-decision
description: User makes a deliberate architectural choice (single-writer SQLite vs. write-pool). Tests whether the model produces a decisions/ ADR-style page distinct from the session log.

observations.title: user prompt
observations.body: I'm seeing 'database is locked' under concurrent writes in the integration tests. should I move to a connection pool with retry, or a single-writer actor?

observations.title: user prompt
observations.body: actually let me think out loud — a pool means rusqlite serialises writes anyway via BEGIN IMMEDIATE retries, and we'd still see SQLITE_BUSY under contention. With a single-writer actor we get FIFO ordering for free, no BUSY, and an obvious place to add audit log.

observations.title: user prompt
observations.body: going with single-writer actor; pool the readers separately.

observations.title: Write
observations.body: crates/engram-store/src/writer.rs: new WriterActor owning one Connection, accepts WriteCmd via tokio::sync::mpsc

observations.title: cargo test
observations.body: concurrent_writes test green; no SQLITE_BUSY under 100 parallel writers

observations.title: user prompt
observations.body: the audit log column was the clincher — we couldn't do it cleanly with a pool
